title: Regimes and Ideologies
shortSummary: Compare democracy, communism, fascism, socialism, and authoritarian rule through the tradeoffs they make around legitimacy, freedom, coordination, and coercion.
whyItMatters: If you cannot distinguish how regimes justify power, you cannot interpret history, current affairs, or the risks inside your own system. This module gives you the high-level map before you drown in details.
coreConcepts: Why every regime promises an upside and carries a failure mode
mentalModels: Regimes are tradeoff systems, not brand labels
Freedom, accountability, speed, and control rarely all move up together
The most important question is what corrects bad power

goDeeper.label: Open the system map
goDeeper.description: Use the flagship visual explainer to keep the whole subject coherent.

goDeeper.label: Jump to Australia's political system
goDeeper.description: Apply the regime comparison to the system you actually live inside.
